meta.note: Canonical brand values. Read these verbatim; never approximate brand colors or type from memory. Contains brand expression only — no commercial or strategic information.

navy.use: Primary dark. Hero and section backgrounds, card bodies, logo plane body on light grounds.

green.use: THE brand green. Fills, icons, highlights, and accents on any ground; large surfaces. NOT for text on light grounds (2.4:1, fails WCAG AA) — use greenInk. On a green fill, text is navy, not white.

greenPunch.use: Punch accent ONLY. At most one intentional emphasis moment per page or asset. Never for buttons, labels, charts, or recurring UI.

greenInk.use: ACCESSIBLE TEXT VARIANT of green — eyebrows, links, inline emphasis on light grounds. 6.2:1 on white, passes WCAG AA. Same hue family as green.

teal.use: Heritage accent. Fills, dark-ground text, data viz series 3. NOT for text on light grounds (2.8:1, fails WCAG AA) — use tealInk.

tealInk.use: ACCESSIBLE TEXT VARIANT of teal — subheads, links, mid-sentence callouts on light grounds. 5.7:1 on white, passes WCAG AA.

steel.use: Secondary blue. Data viz series 2, supporting UI, diagrams. Passes AA as text on white (6.2:1) already.

slate.use: Muted blue-gray. Borders, gridlines, decorative use, text on DARK grounds only. NOT for text on light grounds (2.6:1, fails WCAG AA) — use inkMuted.

inkMuted.use: ACCESSIBLE TEXT VARIANT of slate — captions, secondary labels, chart axis text on light grounds. 5.1:1 on white, passes WCAG AA.

gray.use: Deck system: slide headers and body text on white.

no.use: "Don't"/error fill and borders — decorative use only. NOT for text (3.4:1, fails WCAG AA) — use noInk.

noInk.use: ACCESSIBLE TEXT VARIANT of no — "don't" tags, form error text/messages on light grounds. 6.6:1 on white, passes WCAG AA.

coral.use: ILLUSTRATION ONLY. The glowing path/node accent in paper-cut scenes. Never a UI, chart, or text color.

mint.use: Soft gradient stop A. Light card and panel surfaces.

ice.use: Soft gradient stop B. Pairs with mint at 135 degrees.

white.use: Plane body on navy grounds; light surfaces.

soft.use: Standard light surface for panels, expanded cards, alternating sections.

green.use: Counts as a punch usage — same one-per-asset rule.

wing.use: Official paper-plane wing: deep teal at tail to light green at nose. Motif use only.

primary.use: The brand typeface (Kastelov). Carries every role; weight does the hierarchy work.

fallback.use: Approved free substitute when Axiforma is unavailable. The ONLY acceptable substitute.

sizeScale.note: Every font-size in the system (index.html) resolves to one of these 10 fixed steps, plus the dedicated h1/h2/body/lede sizes above. Consolidated from ~36 unrelated one-off values that had accumulated with no shared scale — every element now references one step, so a future size change happens in one place.

2xs.use: Finest print — nav tagline, deck micro-eyebrow, tiny badges

xs.use: Fine meta text — slide numbers, breadcrumb, footer links

sm.use: Small labels — eyebrows, category-ish small text

md.use: The dominant secondary/caption size across cards and captions

lg.use: Near-body UI text — buttons, form fields, list copy

xl.use: Card titles, sub-headings (equal to the lede size)

5xl.use: The largest decorative glyph (testimonial quote mark)

layout.angleBreak: 2-4 degree diagonal between sections; echoes the plane wing
layout.archetype: navy hero, then alternating navy/soft-gradient sections joined by angled breaks; each section opens with centered eyebrow + H2 + lede

navigation.structure: Two-row layout: logo/wordmark on its own first row, a second row of section-group buttons below it. Each group button (aria-expanded + aria-controls) discloses a subsection dropdown of links — click/keyboard-triggered only, never hover-only. Only one group open at a time; Escape and outside-click/focus-out close it.

dataViz.labels: Axiforma/Poppins, slate for axes, navy for emphasis, 8-11px
dataViz.strokeWeight: 3px with 3px radius on bars and line caps
dataViz.rules: No 3D, no heavy borders, no chart-junk, no background fills. Punch green never appears in charts.

motif.paperPlane: Wing is the gradient blade (#62D38C nose to #30737B tail). Body navy on light grounds, white on navy. Never outlined, never flat green. Use the official SVG paths, not redrawn geometry.
motif.flightPath: Round dots (stroke-dasharray 0.1 8, round caps, ~3px), navy on light / white on navy. Every trail includes at least one loop. Never straight, never dashed. Five canonical variants — A Single loop, B S-wave, C Tight coil, D Wide arc, E Double curl — are defined in the style guide. New paths should follow the same hand-flown character.
motif.icons: Bold navy outlines (~3.5px at 48px grid) with a single flat green fill element. On navy, outlines invert to white; green fills stay green.

accessibility.standard: WCAG 2.2 AA — the baseline for every This Waay and client deliverable, not a follow-up pass.
accessibility.textOnLight: Use greenInk / tealInk / inkMuted / noInk for text on white or soft-gradient grounds. The raw saturated hues (green, teal, slate, no) are fills/large-surfaces/dark-ground text only.
accessibility.textOnGreenOrTealFill: Navy text, never white. White-on-green and white-on-teal measure ~2.4-2.8:1 and fail AA; navy-on-green/teal measures 5.2-6:1 and passes.

focusRing.colorOnDark: greenPunch (green-bright) — greenInk does not have enough contrast on navy grounds

accessibility.structure: One <main> landmark, one <h1>, sequential <h2>s, a skip-to-content link as the first focusable element.
accessibility.keyboardAndWidgets: Every clickable element is a real button/link, or has role + tabindex="0" + a keydown handler. Async state changes announce via aria-live="polite", not a visual-only change. Prefer native elements (button, details/summary, dialog) over custom-built equivalents — they carry keyboard support and semantics for free.
accessibility.imagesAndIcons: Decorative SVGs/icons get aria-hidden="true". Meaningful ones (logo, narrative illustration used as a hero) get role="img" + a real aria-label/alt describing the meaning, not the literal scene.
accessibility.forms: Every field has a visible, programmatically associated <label>. Required fields are marked with text, not an asterisk-only color cue. Errors use aria-describedby + role="alert", not color alone.
accessibility.dataViz: Never distinguish series by color alone — pair color with a line style, marker shape, or a direct label (WCAG 1.4.1).

motion.principle: Purposeful and directional, echoing the flight-path metaphor. Never decorative-for-its-own-sake.

easing.note: No bounce, spring-overshoot, or elastic easing.

motion.signatureMotion: Flight-path draw-on: one-time stroke-dashoffset reveal, ~2.4s ease-out, for hero/section entrances. Never looping.
mustNeverAnimate: autoplaying/looping motion with no pause control (WCAG 2.2.2)
anything flashing more than 3 times per second (WCAG 2.3.1)
motion as the only carrier of meaning
motion.reducedMotionContract: Every new animated component defines its own prefers-reduced-motion fallback next to the animation itself — not reliant on a single global reset alone.
motion.slideDecks: Fade or cut transitions only, no per-slide novelty transitions, always presenter-controlled — never auto-advance.

slideDeck.numbering: bottom-right; omitted on title and closing slides
slideDeck.presenterNotes: below the slide, never on it
slideDeck.minTextSize: ~18px equivalent at 1080p — larger than web body minimums for projected/shared-screen legibility
slideDeck.contrastAndMotion: Same rules as web — see accessibility and motion blocks.
